location.office_time_required: at least 3 days per week (Monday, Wednesday, Thursday); up to 4 weeks remote per year

secondary_archetype: Technical PM (GTM infrastructure / AI-powered revenue systems)

level_signals: Compensation range $240K-$300K base, consistent with Staff IC at a late-stage fintech
'Help define and scale Brex's Growth Product function as it evolves' implies senior scope-setting

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience building and scaling growth, revenue, or GTM systems in a high-growth technology company.
Experience owning and materially moving company-level metrics.
Strong systems thinking — ability to identify leverage points across product, process, and people systems.
Demonstrated track record of leading complex, cross-functional initiatives with executive visibility.
Deep analytical fluency; comfortable working with funnel metrics, experimentation frameworks, and data tools (SQL proficiency preferred).
High ownership mindset with the ability to operate in ambiguity and build from first principles.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Deep analytical fluency; comfortable working with funnel metrics, experimentation frameworks, and data tools (SQL proficiency preferred).

scope.surface: GTM infrastructure and growth systems — AI agents, lifecycle automation, pipeline scoring, onboarding optimization, experimentation frameworks, revenue tooling
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS / fintech — corporate cards, banking, spend management, bill pay, travel software
core_responsibilities: Own a critical GTM-related metric and define the product strategy and roadmap to drive step-change improvements.
Identify structural bottlenecks across the revenue funnel — spanning tooling, automation, data, incentives, and workflows — and design systems to address them.
Lead 0→1 and 1→N initiatives that re-architect Brex's GTM infrastructure.
Partner deeply with Sales, Marketing, RevOps, Engineering, Legal, IT, and Data to drive high-impact outcomes.
Influence stakeholders and executives through clear strategy, strong business cases, and measurable results.
Establish clear input and output metrics, and operate with rigor on experimentation, prioritization, and tradeoffs.
Help define and scale Brex's Growth Product function as it evolves.

compensation.equity_form: equity and other forms of compensation may be provided as part of a total compensation package

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and competitive ($240K-$300K base) for a Staff PM role
Clear product surface with specific initiative examples (AI agents, scoring, onboarding optimization)
Dedicated engineering and design team paired with this PM role
Explicit GTM metric ownership — high business impact and visibility
Brex has notable enterprise customers (DoorDash, Coinbase, Robinhood, Zoom, Plaid, Reddit, SeatGeek)
red_flags: Hybrid mandate is strict — 3 fixed days per week (Mon/Wed/Thu) in SF, Seattle, or NYC; only 4 weeks remote per year
SQL proficiency is listed as 'preferred' in requirements but is also included as a hard requirement bullet — ambiguous severity
No years-of-experience floor stated, making seniority calibration difficult
Visa sponsorship status not mentioned anywhere in the JD
High buzzword density (AI-native, AI agents, autonomous, real-time signals) with limited specifics on current system maturity

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from role title 'Staff' and compensation range)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from JD description; not listed as explicit targets)
compensation.location_pay_differential (inferred from 'starting base pay will depend on candidate's location')
ambiguities: SQL proficiency appears in the Requirements section labeled as 'preferred' within the same sentence as hard analytical requirements — treated as nice-to-have (soft) but the surrounding sentence reads as a hard requirement; both must_haves and nice_to_haves capture this.
The JD does not specify whether the role manages direct reports; inferred as individual contributor (manages_people: false) based on 'Staff PM' framing and no mention of people management.
Years of experience required not stated anywhere; seniority inferred from title and compensation.
